title: pastinaca glandulosa
intro: Pastinaca glandulosa, also known as wild parsnip, is a biennial or perennial plant species in the carrot family that is native to Europe and western Asia. The medicinal properties of Pastinaca glandulosa include its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as urinary issues and skin irritations. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to support kidney function, reduce swelling and pain, and promote wound healing. Pastinaca glandulosa can be prepared as an herbal remedy through teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used topically as a poultice or ointment to apply directly to affected areas.
